summary: Frontend E2E capacity enforcement testing for Zenith jar. All 9 functional flow steps PASSED end-to-end on the live preview URL. Starting from clean slate (0/100), added 3 Stones sequentially -> 30/60/90, attempted Pebble (fail), attempted Stone (fail) both with 'Jar is full! Remove or ship existing tasks first.' error toast (data-type='error'). Backend correctly returned 400 on overflow. Added Sand (5) -> 95/100 success. Deleted Sand -> freed capacity back to 90/100. Re-added Sand -> 95/100 succeeded confirming delete-frees-capacity flow. Final cleanup removed all test tasks back to 0/100. Step 10 (JAR NEARLY FULL warning) is rendered on a <canvas> via ctx.fillText in JarCanvas.jsx (line 112-116) when fillRatio > 0.8 — code path is correct but cannot be asserted via DOM inner_text since canvas pixels are not DOM text. Code review confirms the logic is wired correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: JarCanvas.jsx: JAR NEARLY FULL warning is drawn only on canvas (ctx.fillText). Consider also exposing a DOM sibling element (visually-hidden or aria-live) with data-testid='jar-nearly-full' for accessibility and testability. Currently it is verifiable only via pixel snapshot.
TaskPanel.jsx: After a failed add (jar full), the input is correctly retained (good UX) and the error toast is shown — verified.
useGameState addTask error handling surfaces the backend's detail string verbatim via toast.error which is clean; good error propagation.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (9/9 functional steps; step 10 canvas-only verified via code review)

seed_data_creation: Tasks created with 'TEST ...' titles during the run were deleted in final cleanup; jar returned to 0/100.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Jar capacity enforcement flow fully verified E2E on the live preview URL. Key behaviors confirmed: (1) POST /api/tasks returns 400 when current_vol + new_vol > 100, (2) frontend useGameState catches error and shows sonner toast with backend detail, (3) DELETE /api/tasks/{id} correctly frees volume in session (immediately reflected in data-testid='volume-meter'). Test script used data-testid selectors: task-type-trigger + type-stone/pebble/sand (force click), task-input, add-task-btn, delete-btn-{id}, volume-meter. Waited ~1.8s after each add for API round-trip. Toast selector [data-sonner-toast] with data-type='error' attribute works well for error-toast assertion.
rca of the issue: N/A - no bugs found. Full-capacity enforcement + delete-frees-capacity behavior works as specified.
